title: 10 best herbal essential oils for chapped lips
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for chapped lips are natural remedies extracted from plants that help to soothe, moisturize, and protect dry, cracked lips. These oils contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that provide relief from discomfort and irritation. The best herbal essential oils for chapped lips include Aloe barbadensis, Mentha x piperita, Cocos nucifera, Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Vitis vinifera, Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe vera, and Ricinus communis. If left untreated, chapped lips can lead to discomfort, difficulty eating, and even emotional distress, negatively impacting daily life and social interactions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its remarkable moisturizing properties. The oil's soothing compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to calm dryness and inflammation, leaving lips feeling soft and supple. Additionally, the oil's natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing. When applied topically, English lavender essential oils provide long-lasting relief from chapped, cracked, or irritated lips, leaving them looking healthy and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Herbal English Lavender essential oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause infection and dryness in the lips, promoting healing and soothing.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in English Lavender essential oil reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with chapped lips, providing relief and comfort.
Moisturizing Properties: The essential oil's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and hydrate the lips, reducing dryness and cracking while promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Flowers: Contain linalool, a natural moisturizer that helps lock in moisture and protect against further drying.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves in the early morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested leaves for 2 weeks by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Steam distill the dried lavandula angustifolia leaves with water at a ratio of 1:5 for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the final lavender essential oil in an airtight container away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its natural soothing and moisturizing properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to calm and relax dry, chapped skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, the antiseptic and antibacterial properties of peppermint oil help to prevent infection and promote healthy healing. When applied topically, peppermint essential oils can provide instant relief from dryness and irritation, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Peppermint essential oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections and inflammation on the lips, promoting a healthy environment for healing and reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint essential oil reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chapped lips, providing relief and soothing dry, irritated skin.
Moisturizing property: Peppermint essential oil's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and hydrate the lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, a natural emollient that helps to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Contain menthone, a natural analgesic that reduces inflammation and provides relief from lip discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves in the morning after dew has dried for maximum potency and freshness.
Wash the mint leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and contaminants.
Dry the mint leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air dry them for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried mint leaves in a clevenger apparatus using 10-12 ounces of water at 145°f for 2-3 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distilled mixture through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The oil's antimicrobial compounds work to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections that can cause dry, cracked lips. Additionally, lemon balm's natural astringent properties help to tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. By applying a few drops of lemon balm essential oil to the affected area, you can experience immediate relief from dry, chapped lips and enjoy a softer, smoother pout.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry and chapped lips.
Antimicrobial: Lemon balm essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause lip dryness, cracks, and irritation.
Moisturizing: The essential oil has moisturizing properties that can help lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin surface.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective treatment for soothing and healing dry, cracked lips.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain compounds that help to calm and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn and dry them immediately in a warm place for 24 hours.
Blend one cup of dried melissa officinalis leaves with one cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steam distill the blend using a still or a steam distillation kit for 30-60 minutes to extract essential oils.
Filter the extracted essential oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and collect the clear liquid in a dark glass bottle.
Store the final melissa officinalis essential oil in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and calming properties. When applied topically, it provides instant relief from dryness and irritation by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile oil also reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing abilities help to lock in hydration and promote healing. As a result, chapped lips are quickly comforted and restored to their natural softness and smoothness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling caused by chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile essential oil help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate chapped lips, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile essential oil has a soothing and calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce dryness and irritation associated with chapped lips, and providing instant relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds, chamomile flowers help to calm and comfort dry, chapped lips.
Roots: With their high concentration of antioxidants and vitamins, chamomile roots promote healthy skin and lip tissue, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 freshly dried chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 200ml of high-proof vodka or everclear to the jar, ensuring complete coverage of the flowers.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the liquid to mature for an additional 2 weeks before filtering again and transferring to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its unique properties that soothe and moisturize dry, cracked skin. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling, while its emollients lock in moisture to soften and hydrate the lips. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, promoting healthy and supple lip tissue. By using pot marigold essential oils, individuals can enjoy soft, smooth, and nourished lips throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of calendula oil can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing, which can be beneficial for chapped or cracked lips.
Emollient: Calendula oil is rich in fatty acids that can help to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips by locking in moisture and protecting the skin from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm chapped lips.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in antioxidants that protect the lips from further dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Rinse the collected flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris gently pat dry.
Steam distill the dried flower petals using a clevenger apparatus or similar equipment for 4-6 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the steam condensate using a paper coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles.
Store the calendula officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use to mature flavor and aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its extraordinary moisturizing properties. The oil is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which penetrate deep into the skin to soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips. Additionally, damask rose has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips. By using a lip balm or oil infused with damask rose essential oil, you can experience immediate relief from chapped, cracked lips and enjoy soft, supple, and healthy-looking lips all year round.
remedy_properties: Emollient: Damask rose essential oil has emollient properties, which means it helps to fill in the gaps between skin cells and lock in moisture, providing relief from dry and chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in damask rose essential oil can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting healing and soothing irritated skin.
Antioxidant: Damask rose essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate lip dryness and chapping.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and emollients, damask rose flowers help to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Roots: With anti-inflammatory properties, damask rose roots can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa damascena flowers at peak bloom for optimal oil quality and fragrance.
Dry fresh flowers thoroughly in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Steam distill dried flowers with distilled water in a stainless steel still at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the essential oil produced during steam distillation in a separate container for storage and use.
Store rosa damascena essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its aroma and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its natural soothing properties. The oil's unique composition, rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, provides instant relief to dry, cracked, or irritated lips. When applied topically, the aloe oil forms a protective barrier that locks in moisture, calming inflammation and reducing pain. Its anti-inflammatory agents also help to promote healthy lip tissue regeneration, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Sobering and anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's essential oil helps to reduce redness and inflammation, providing relief from irritated and chapped lips.
Antioxidant and anti-aging properties: Herbal aloe essential oil contains antioxidants that combat free radicals and promote collagen production, helping to soothe and regenerate damaged lip tissue.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Aloe vera's essential oil provides intense hydration and nourishment to dry, chapped lips, locking in moisture and softening the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that help to calm and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Roots: Contain potent antioxidant properties that can help to repair and protect damaged skin tissue on the lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from the plant and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Blend 3 tablespoons of chopped aloe vera gel with 6 tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 5 minutes.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and mix well to enhance skin penetration and antioxidant properties.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f to 180°f for 45 minutes to allow the essential oils to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, thyme oil soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. Its antioxidant compounds also help to lock in moisture, hydrating dry and chapped lips. Additionally, the gentle, non-irritating nature of thyme essential oil makes it an excellent natural remedy for chapped lips, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and promoting healthy lip tissue.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme essential oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat infections and inflammation caused by chapped lips, reducing redness and soreness.
Wound-healing properties: Thyme oil's ability to promote wound healing can accelerate the repair of dry, cracked skin on the lips, helping to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chapped and dry lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory essential oil that helps to calm and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have antibacterial properties, making them effective at combating infections and promoting healing of dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a pesticide-free environment ensuring accurate dosing.
Wash the harvested plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and contaminants.
Steam distill the cleaned thymus vulgaris material using 10g of botanicals per liter of water for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates during distillation into a separate container within 1 hour.
Filter and bottle the obtained essential oil using a coffee filter paper and dark glass bottles with tight lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its remarkable moisturizing properties. The oil's natural emollients deeply penetrate the skin, soothing and hydrating dry, cracked lips. Its anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce redness and irritation, while its antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote healing. Additionally, the oil's refreshing scent can help to stimulate saliva production, further contributing to the hydration of the lips. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils provide effective relief from chapped and dry lips.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Eucalyptus oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to chapped or cracked lips, promoting a healthy environment for wound healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol and beta-caryophyllene, which may help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with chapped lips, allowing the skin to recover faster.
Wound-healing properties: The antioxidant and antimicrobial activities of eucalyptus oil can aid in wound healing by protecting the skin from further damage and promoting the growth of new tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm chapped lips.
Barks: Contains antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing of cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content and minimal contamination.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a traditional steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure and a temperature of 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water phase as a result of the distillation process and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Mix the collected essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10% eucalyptus globulus to 90% carrier oil by volume for safe application on lips.
Store the prepared lip balm in an airtight container and use within 6 months, applying it gently to chapped lips twice daily for optimal relief and soothing benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with chapped lips because of its natural moisturizing and nourishing properties. The oil is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and fatty acids that help to soothe and hydrate dry, cracked lips. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and irritation, promoting a smooth and soft appearance. Additionally, sesame oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria and fungi that can cause lip infections, leaving lips feeling healthy and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sesame essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
Antioxidant: Sesame oil's antioxidant properties protect the skin from environmental stressors, such as dry air and harsh weather conditions, which can exacerbate lip dryness and irritation.
Emollient: Sesame oil's emollient properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, sesame roots help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: Sesame seeds contain natural emollients and anti-inflammatory compounds that ease lip irritation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sesamum indicum seeds and dry them in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Crush dried sesamum indicum seeds into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of sesamum indicum powder with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in a dark, cool place for three weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to airtight containers for storage and use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chapped lips with herbal essential oils is by blending a few drops of soothing lavender oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the mixture directly to the affected area, massaging gently until fully absorbed. Alternatively, add a few drops of peppermint oil to your lip balm or chapstick for an invigorating and cooling effect that helps to lock in moisture and reduce dryness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chapped lips that are treated with herbal essential oils are dry skin conditions, cracked heels, and eczema.
